# track-id 引数を省略可能にし、省略時は現在ブランチに紐づくアクティブトラックを既定値とする

## Tasks (13/14 resolved)

### S1 — usecase: port + interactor for active-track resolution

> Introduce the BranchReaderPort secondary port and all associated types (BranchReadError, ActiveTrackResolveError, ActiveTrackResolveService, ActiveTrackResolveInteractor) in libs/usecase/src/track_resolution.rs.
> Re-use the existing resolve_track_id_from_branch for the parse rule so no new resolution logic is introduced (IN-04, IN-05, CN-04, CN-05).
> Include unit tests using a stub BranchReaderPort to verify the resolution cases (track branch, non-track branch, detached HEAD via Some("HEAD"), and no branch via None) without git I/O (IN-10, AC-08).

- [x] **T001**: usecase: add BranchReaderPort, BranchReadError, ActiveTrackResolveError, ActiveTrackResolveService, ActiveTrackResolveInteractor to libs/usecase/src/track_resolution.rs. BranchReaderPort is the secondary port (current_branch() -> Result<Option<String>, BranchReadError>), passing through Some("HEAD") for detached HEAD and using None only when no branch name can be determined. ActiveTrackResolveInteractor holds Arc<dyn BranchReaderPort> and delegates to the existing resolve_track_id_from_branch for the parse rule (IN-04, IN-05). Implement standard trait impls for both error types (Debug, Display, Error, From). Add unit tests for the new types: (a) track/<id> branch resolves correctly, (b) main branch returns NotTrackBranch, (c) detached HEAD (Some("HEAD") from current_branch) returns DetachedHead, and (d) None from current_branch returns NoBranch. These tests use a stub BranchReaderPort — no git I/O (IN-10, AC-08). Keep changes within libs/usecase/src/track_resolution.rs (CN-04, CN-05). (`dffe739b287035812229683cdf30d588c6bbe996`)

### S2 — infrastructure: SystemGitRepo implements BranchReaderPort

> Add the BranchReaderPort trait impl for SystemGitRepo in libs/infrastructure/src/git_cli/mod.rs by delegating to the existing GitRepository::current_branch() method and mapping the error to BranchReadError::ReadFailed.
> This completes the port inversion: the usecase layer declares the port; the infrastructure layer provides the adapter (IN-04, IN-06, CN-05).

- [x] **T002**: infrastructure: add BranchReaderPort impl for SystemGitRepo in libs/infrastructure/src/git_cli/mod.rs. SystemGitRepo::current_branch() already exists on GitRepository; wire it as the BranchReaderPort impl by delegating to that method and mapping the error type to BranchReadError::ReadFailed (IN-04, IN-06, CN-05). Ensure the trait impl is exported from the infrastructure crate public API. Keep changes within libs/infrastructure/src/git_cli/ (one impl block). This task is a prerequisite for T003 (CLI wiring) and T004 (write-guard integration). (`e66ec101dd214706e7a7fd477fecc52607e8cf44`)

### S3 — usecase: replace write-guard closure injection with BranchReaderPort

> TaskOperationInteractor currently injects a raw closure that shells out to git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD for branch validation (active-track-write-guard pattern).
> Replace the closure parameter with Arc<dyn BranchReaderPort> and update the CLI call sites (transition.rs, state_ops.rs) to pass SystemGitRepo (IN-07, CN-05).
> This consolidates validation and default resolution onto the same port-based path as T001-T002, eliminating the remaining direct git shelling-out from usecase-facing handlers.

- [x] **T003**: usecase: replace the BranchReaderFn closure injection in TaskOperationInteractor with Arc<dyn BranchReaderPort>. TaskOperationInteractor::new currently accepts a closure `move |_items_dir| { Command::new("git").args(["rev-parse","--abbrev-ref","HEAD"]) ... }` for the branch guard (IN-07, CN-05). Replace this with Arc<dyn BranchReaderPort> as the second constructor parameter. Update the internal branch-reading call to invoke BranchReaderPort::current_branch() instead. Update all call sites in apps/cli/src/commands/track/ (transition.rs, state_ops.rs) to pass Arc::new(SystemGitRepo::discover_from(...)) as the BranchReaderPort. This removes the last direct git rev-parse shelling-out from usecase-facing command handlers. Keep changes within libs/usecase/src/task_ops.rs plus the CLI call sites that wire the interactor (IN-07, CN-05). (`9e8b31aba10e2a5501e043c4f83db11f033d0626`)

### S7 — Makefile: shared track-active-gate task + bare chain wrappers (D4 gate symmetry, DRY regen sequence)

> Introduce a single shared cargo make task (track-active-gate) that defines the regen sequence exactly once: sotp track type-signals → sotp track catalogue-spec-signals → sotp track views sync (CN-11).
> Rewrite cargo make track-local-review to call track-active-gate as a dependency then invoke sotp review local; rewrite the track-commit-message pre-commit regen step to call track-active-gate as a dependency before the commit action (IN-11, IN-12).
> Both wrappers must chain fail-closed (CN-09); neither may contain its own copy of the regen sequence or any BRANCH= / TRACK_ID= shell variables (CN-10).
> The bare-chain refactor of track-commit-message changes only the regen invocation form; the full cargo make ci check and check-approved (fail-closed BLOCK) are preserved without weakening (CN-12).
> Depends on T004 and T005 (sotp commands must self-resolve track-id before the wrappers can omit it). Satisfies IN-11, IN-12, CN-09, CN-10, CN-11, CN-12, AC-11, AC-12, AC-13.

- [x] **T008**: Makefile gate symmetry (D4): define a single shared cargo make task (e.g., track-active-gate) that encodes the regen sequence — sotp track type-signals → sotp track catalogue-spec-signals → sotp track views sync — exactly once. Rewrite cargo make track-local-review to call track-active-gate as a dependency then invoke sotp review local; rewrite the cargo make track-commit-message pre-commit regen step to call track-active-gate as a dependency before the commit action. Both wrappers must chain with fail-closed semantics (CN-09): non-zero exit from any step aborts the chain (AC-13). Neither wrapper may contain its own copy of the regen sequence or any BRANCH= / TRACK_ID= shell variables — duplication of the chain between the two wrappers is structurally prohibited by routing both through the shared task (CN-10, CN-11). The bare-chain refactor changes only the regen invocation form; the cargo make track-commit-message commit gate retains its full strict checks: complete cargo make ci and check-approved (review not approved → fail-closed BLOCK). These strict checks are NOT removed or weakened (CN-12). This task depends on T004 and T005 (which make the chained sotp commands accept optional track-id so each command self-resolves via the current branch). Satisfies IN-11, IN-12, CN-09, CN-10, CN-11, CN-12, AC-11, AC-12, AC-13. (`3b997a2f080a51c12859d08d1b2b684f23596e04`)

- [x] **T009**: Operational-docs cleanup: remove explicit --track-id from documentation where commands now self-resolve. Edit the following files to drop examples, step descriptions, and command invocations that pass an explicit --track-id (or equivalent positional track_id) to commands that now self-resolve from the current branch: .claude/commands/track/*.md (all affected command docs), track/workflow.md, DEVELOPER_AI_WORKFLOW.md, and Makefile.toml task description comments. ### S11 — CLI: non-track branch skip for CI verify checks (AC-16 fix)

> Add CLI control-flow in apps/cli/src/commands/verify.rs so that the four ci-local CI verify subcommands (verify spec-states / verify catalogue-spec-refs / verify catalogue-spec-signals / verify plan-artifact-refs) skip with [SKIP] and exit 0 when no active track is resolvable (NotTrackBranch / DetachedHead / NoBranch).
> verify catalogue-spec-signals currently resolves the track inside infrastructure; wire ActiveTrackResolveInteractor at the CLI dispatch layer so the non-track skip is uniform across all four subcommands.
> Real failures (BranchRead / InvalidTrackId / etc.) and operation commands (transition, signals, etc.) stay fail-closed — the skip applies only to the four CI-gate verify commands on non-track branches (CN-01 documented exception).
> No new types. Add unit tests for the skip path using a stub BranchReaderPort (AC-16).

- [x] **T012**: CLI: fix the main-CI regression for AC-16 — make the four ci-local CI verify checks (verify spec-states / verify catalogue-spec-refs / verify catalogue-spec-signals / verify plan-artifact-refs) skip (exit 0, print a [SKIP] message) on a non-track branch (no active track resolvable), so cargo make ci passes on main. Implement as a CLI control-flow change in apps/cli/src/commands/verify.rs per the Phase-2 design: when active-track resolution returns the not-a-track-branch error family (NotTrackBranch / DetachedHead / NoBranch), emit [SKIP] and return ExitCode::SUCCESS; real failures (BranchRead / InvalidTrackId / etc.) stay fail-closed. verify catalogue-spec-signals currently resolves the track inside infrastructure — add a CLI-dispatch conditional using ActiveTrackResolveInteractor (same pattern as the other three verify subcommands) to skip on no-track before delegating to the infrastructure path. Operation commands (transition, signals, etc.) and direct invocations with an explicit-but-invalid track keep fail-closed (D1 / CN-01). No new types; this is pure CLI control-flow. Add unit tests for the skip path: stub BranchReaderPort returning a non-track branch name or None to verify all four subcommands emit [SKIP] and exit 0. Do NOT reintroduce shell branch-parsing in the Makefile (AC-11). Covers AC-16, within CN-01's documented exception. (`5b786cb3801a8cd72630c3bf61ad3a4c794d5d29`)

### S13 — make.rs wrapper passthrough: remove positional track-id consumption + update call sites

> Rewrite the five cargo make task wrappers (dispatch_track_add_task / dispatch_track_transition / dispatch_track_next_task / dispatch_track_task_counts / dispatch_track_set_override) in apps/cli/src/commands/make.rs to stop consuming a positional track-id. Four of the five (dispatch_track_add_task / dispatch_track_transition / dispatch_track_next_task / dispatch_track_task_counts) inject --items-dir track/items and forward all remaining args verbatim to the underlying sotp command. dispatch_track_set_override is the exception: it additionally reads the first non-flag positional word as the status (blocked / cancelled / clear) to route to either track set-override or track clear-override; this routing is necessary because the single Makefile entry track-set-override covers two distinct underlying sotp commands.
> dispatch_track_transition currently extracts track_dir from the first positional arg and derives both --items-dir and --track-id from it; change it to inject --items-dir track/items and passthrough --track-id when the caller provides it explicitly.
> Update all existing call sites that currently pass a positional track-id or track_dir to these wrappers: .claude/commands/track/implement.md, .claude/commands/track/full-cycle.md, knowledge/conventions/task-completion-flow.md, and apps/cli/src/commands/make_tests.rs (IN-15, AC-17, D6).

- [ ] **T014**: make.rs wrapper passthrough: remove positional track-id consumption from the five cargo make task wrappers and update all existing call sites. (1) In apps/cli/src/commands/make.rs, rewrite dispatch_track_add_task, dispatch_track_transition, dispatch_track_next_task, dispatch_track_task_counts, and dispatch_track_set_override so that they no longer consume a positional track-id. Four of the five (add_task, transition, next_task, task_counts) inject --items-dir track/items and forward all remaining args verbatim. dispatch_track_set_override is the exception: it additionally reads the first non-flag positional word as the status (blocked / cancelled / clear) to route to either track set-override or track clear-override (the single Makefile entry covers two underlying commands), and it does NOT consume a positional track-id. dispatch_track_transition currently extracts track_dir from the first positional arg and derives --items-dir and --track-id by splitting it; change it to inject --items-dir track/items and pass --track-id only when explicitly present in the forwarded args (i.e. passthrough). After this change, cargo make track-add-task -- "write docs" resolves the active track from the current branch (D1 self-resolve), and cargo make track-add-task -- --track-id <id> "write docs" uses the explicit id. (2) Update existing call sites that still pass a positional track-id or track_dir to the five wrappers: .claude/commands/track/implement.md, .claude/commands/track/full-cycle.md, knowledge/conventions/task-completion-flow.md (cargo make track-transition <track_dir> <task_id> <status> invocation pattern), and apps/cli/src/commands/make_tests.rs (positional wrapper tests). Change each to the --track-id flag form or to the track-id-omitted form as appropriate. No new types. Covers IN-15, AC-17.
